content.full_text:
12 April 1977

Honorable Claude B. Brown
Judge of the Municipal Court
Desert Judicial District
Drawer BBB
Indio, California 92201

Dear Judge Brown:

Thank you for your letter of April 7, 1977 concerning
whether you are required to report benefits received
from the San Diego County Employees Retirement Associ-
ation.

The Commission is currently reviewing the question of
whether or not payments or benefits received from pension
and retirement funds are reportable as income on the fi-
nancial disclosure statement.

In lieu of an official opinion rendered by the Commission,
the staff advice to filers of Form 721 is that pension or
retirement benefits payments from public (i.e., federal,
state or local government) sources need not be disclosed
but payments of a similar type from private sector benefit
plans should be reported.

If the Commission's decision on the question is contrary
to the interim advice, you will be notified. However, any-
one who files a statement conforming with the interim advice
will be deemed to be in compliance with the law.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchina
Program Coordinator

RSK/gt


bcc: Jeanne P.

CLAUDE B. BROWN JUDGE
INDIO DEPARTMENT
Telephone (714) 347-4511

The Municipal Court

DESERT JUDICIAL DISTRICT
COUNTY OF RIVERSIDE, CALIFORNIA

April 7, 1977

82-675 AVENUE 46
DRAWER BBB
INDIO, CALIFORNIA 92201


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

Your opinion is requested as to whether or not
income from a pension needs to be reported.

For fourteen years I was an employee of the
County of San Diego, Office of the District Attorney.
A contribution to the retirement fund was taken from
each pay check and the County matched that contribution.
These contributions were made by me and the County for
13 years, 10 months. I am now receiving a monthly
retirement check from the San Diego County Employees
Retirement Association. The fund is administered
by that Association and I have no control over the
investments made by it and, in fact, no knowledge
of the nature of the investments.

It has been my interpretation that since that
is a government agency, it is not necessary for the
income from that retirement fund to be declared.
If I am in error, please advise me.

Thank you for your assistance and cooperation.

Yours very truly,

Claude B. Brown
Judge of the Municipal Court
Desert Judicial District

I am now receiving a monthly retirement check from the San Diego County Employees Retirement Association. The fund is administered by that Association and I have no control over the investments made by it and, in fact, no knowledge of the nature of the investments. It has been my interpretation that since that is a government agency, it is not necessary for the income from that retirement fund to be declared. If I am in error, please advise me.
sections.conclusion: The Commission is currently reviewing the question of whether or not payments or benefits received from pension and retirement funds are reportable as income on the financial disclosure statement. In lieu of an official opinion rendered by the Commission, the staff advice to filers of Form 721 is that pension or retirement benefits payments from public (i.e., federal, state or local government) sources need not be disclosed but payments of a similar type from private sector benefit plans should be reported. If the Commission's decision on the question is contrary to the interim advice, you will be notified. However, anyone who files a statement conforming with the interim advice will be deemed to be in compliance with the law.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a municipal court judge report pension and retirement benefits received from the San Diego County Employees Retirement Association on financial disclosure statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC interim staff advice indicates that pension/retirement benefits from public sector sources (federal, state, or local government) need not be reported on Form 721, while similar payments from private sector benefit plans should be reported. Filers following this interim advice will be deemed compliant with the law, though the Commission's final decision may differ.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated April 7, 1977) and FPPC response (dated April 12, 1977). OCR quality issues present in some sections but core question and conclusion clearly identifiable. Marked as 'interim advice' rather than final official opinion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your opinion is requested as to whether or not income from a pension needs to be reported? For fourteen years I was an employee of the County of San Diego, Office of the District Attorney. A contribution to the retirement fund was taken from each pay check and the County matched that contribution. These contributions were made by me and the county for 13 years, 10 months. I am now receiving a monthly retirement check from the San Diego County Employees Retirement Association. The fund is administered by that Association and I have no control over the investments made by it and, in fact, no knowledge of the nature of the investments. It has been my interpretation that since that is a government agency, it is not necessary for the income from that retirement fund to be declared. If I am in error, please advise me.

CONCLUSION: The Commission is currently reviewing the question of whether or not payments or benefits received from pension and retirement funds are reportable as income on the financial disclosure statement. In lieu of an official opinion rendered by the Commission, the staff advice to filers of Form 721 is that pension or retirement benefits payments from public (i.e., federal, state or local government) sources need not be disclosed but payments of a similar type from private sector benefit plans should be reported. If the Commission's decision on the question is contrary to the interim advice, you will be notified. However, anyone who files a statement conforming with the interim advice will be deemed to be in compliance with the law.

embedding.summary: Judge Claude B. Brown inquired whether he must report pension income from the San Diego County Employees Retirement Association. The FPPC responded with interim staff advice that public sector pension/retirement benefits need not be disclosed on Form 721, but private sector similar benefits should be reported.
